title: Card weaving pattern sequences
subtitle: How to plan and weave two-colour pattern sequences on card-weaving tablets
excerpt: Card weaving uses a set of square tablets with holes at each corner to hold warp threads. Rotating the tablets in forward and backward sequences builds up a warp-twined band with colour patterns determined by the turning sequence. This guide covers the threading setup and basic pattern sequences for a two-colour band.
sourceNotes: Card weaving is documented in archaeological textile finds dating from the Bronze Age and is taught by the UK Braid Society and in guild publications. The turning-sequence method described is consistent across modern instruction sources.

glossaryTerms.definition: A technique in which a set of square tablets with holes punched at each corner is threaded with warp threads. The warp is held under tension and the cards are rotated in sequence to twist the warp threads around each other, creating a dense, patterned band. Also called tablet weaving.

The pattern of forward and backward card rotations used in card weaving to control colour placement and twist buildup. A simple sequence of four turns forward, four turns back produces a symmetrical diamond or stripe pattern. More complex sequences produce diagonal or twill-based patterns.

A category of narrow weaving producing belts, straps, trim, and decorative bands. Card weaving is one form of band weaving; others include inkle weaving and backstrap weaving. Band weaving is typically warp-faced: only the warp colour is visible on the surface.

content.text: , the pattern is not created by a loom shedding mechanism but by the rotation of individual cards. Each card carries four warp threads, one through each corner hole. As the cards turn, those threads twist around each other; a weft passed through the shed at each turn locks the twist in place and builds the band. This makes card weaving a form of
that requires no loom beyond a simple warp under tension between two fixed points. The pattern is controlled entirely by the
chosen for each set of cards.

items.name: Square weaving cards or tablets with a hole at each corner, set of 12 to 24
Smooth, strong warp yarn: 3/2 cotton or 2-ply wool, colour A
Smooth, strong warp yarn, colour B
Weft yarn matching colour A or neutral, thin
Two fixed anchor points (door handles, G-clamps, or a backstrap setup)

content.text: Each card has four holes labelled A, B, C, D (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). For a simple two-colour stripe pattern, thread 12 cards with the following setup: cards 1 to 6 threaded ABCD with colour A in holes A and D, colour B in holes B and C; cards 7 to 12 threaded ABCD with the colours reversed. Thread all cards from the same direction (front to back) to start. Stack the threaded cards in order, tie the warp ends together at each end, and set the warp under firm, even tension between two fixed points.

Hold all cards together in one hand and rotate them toward you (forward) as a pack: this opens the shed between the two upper threads and the two lower threads of each card. Pass the weft through the shed and pull it snug. Rotate all cards forward again for the next row. After 4 forward turns, reverse and rotate all cards backward for 4 turns, then return to forward. The 4-forward, 4-back sequence prevents the warp threads from building up excessive twist, which would stiffen the band and make the cards hard to turn. Vary the block width (2, 4, or 6 turns per direction) to change the pattern scale.

As weaving progresses, the warp threads between the cards and the far anchor point accumulate twist. When the twist builds to the point that the warp wants to coil on itself, slide the cards toward the far anchor, unwind the warp by rotating the entire card pack several full turns in the opposite direction of accumulated twist, then slide the cards back to the working position. Regular untwisting (every 20 to 30 rows) keeps the tension even and the shed clean.

items.problem: The pattern is not symmetrical and the colours look scrambled
items.cause: Some cards were threaded in the opposite direction to the rest, so they produce the mirror-image twist
items.fix: Check each card: all should be threaded from the same direction (all front-to-back or all back-to-front). A card threaded differently from its neighbours will produce reversed colour in its thread group. Remove, re-thread, and replace.

items.problem: The shed is closing and the weft cannot pass through cleanly
items.cause: Twist has built up to the point where the card pack is locked and the threads cannot separate
items.fix: Stop and untwist the warp by sliding the cards toward the far anchor and rotating the pack in the reverse direction until the warp hangs free. Reduce the number of turns before the next untwisting stop.
